findings.criterion: The residual unsigned-close message (if any) names an ACTUALLY-WORKING recovery, not a no-op re-close.
findings.detail: The drift-warning message at transition_close.py:688-692 still reads: "not attesting stale state. Re-close to certify against the current tree." A re-close of an already-closed ticket is a no-op: txn.transition_core (line 229-241) raises ConcurrencyMismatch when the ticket's actual status is 'closed' and current_status is 'closed'. This warning can still appear in the fallback code path (lines 599-606): if resolve_ref("HEAD") raises, ref stays None, the drift guard falls to the else branch at line 685 and calls head_sha() — recreating the original lazy-read race. In that edge-case scenario, the suggested 'Re-close' remains a no-op, just as the bug report documented. The message was not updated to name a working alternative (e.g. reopen → re-close, or a 'rebar reverify-completion' command).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
